title: GJMSComposition
summary: Specializes ordered compositions to the canonical ambient GJMS family.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specializes ordered compositions to the canonical ambient GJMS family.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows Juhlformula/Juhloperators/GJMSComposition.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The canonical GJMS family, viewed as a Juhl abstract family. In explicit terms, for h of type cal conf.algebraic identities, its defining expression is fun k hk maps to GJMS.canonical Ambient GJMS Cal Conf H k.

The canonical GJMS composition associated to a composition I. Mathematically: P_{2I} = P_{2I_1} ∘... ∘ P_{2I_r}. In explicit terms, for h of type cal conf.algebraic identities, n in the natural numbers, and i of type composition n, its defining expression is compose GJMS Cal Conf canonical Juhl GJMSFamily Cal Conf H I.
correspondence.leanType: def canonicalGJMSComposition (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) {N : Nat} (I : Composition N) : Function Conf → Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, N in the natural numbers, and hN of type 0 < N, canonical GJMSComposition Cal Conf H Composition.singleton N h N equals abstract To Unary Cal Conf GJMS.canonical Ambient GJMS Cal Conf H N.
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalGJMSComposition_singleton (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : canonicalGJMSComposition CalConf H (Composition.singleton N hN) = abstractToUnary CalConf (GJMS.canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H N)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, N in the natural numbers, hN of type 0 < N, and f of type Function Conf, canonical GJMSComposition Cal Conf H Composition.singleton N h N f equals GJMS.canonical Ambient GJMS Cal Conf H N.to Weighted Tangential Operator.to Operator f.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;N\colon \mathbb{N},\;hN\colon 0 < N,\;f\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{canonicalGJMSComposition} \operatorname{CalConf} H (\operatorname{Composition}.\operatorname{singleton} N \operatorname{hN}) f = (\operatorname{GJMS}.\operatorname{canonicalAmbientGJMS} \operatorname{CalConf} H N).\operatorname{toWeightedTangentialOperator}.\operatorname{toOperator} f
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalGJMSComposition_singleton_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) (f : Function Conf) : canonicalGJMSComposition CalConf H (Composition.singleton N hN) f = (GJMS.canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H N).toWeightedTangentialOperator.toOperator f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For H of type Cal Conf.Algebraic Identities, N in the natural numbers, hN of type 0 < N, and f of type Function Conf, canonical GJMSComposition Cal Conf H Composition.singleton N h N f equals Cal Conf.power of the ambient Laplacian N f.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,H\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{AlgebraicIdentities},\;N\colon \mathbb{N},\;hN\colon 0 < N,\;f\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\quad \operatorname{canonicalGJMSComposition} \operatorname{CalConf} H (\operatorname{Composition}.\operatorname{singleton} N \operatorname{hN}) f = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{lapPow} N f
correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalGJMSComposition_singleton_apply_lapPow (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) (f : Function Conf) : canonicalGJMSComposition CalConf H (Composition.singleton N hN) f = CalConf.lapPow N f

limitations: Verified means that Lean checks the propositions encoded in this file and their imported assumptions. It does not assert that the present formal statement exhausts every analytic hypothesis or convention in the cited literature.
